evals.prompt: Take a look at https://northstar.example and tell me what is holding back its search performance.
evals.expected_output: Selects site audit mode with a bounded initial scope because the URL is supplied without goals, audience, market, or business context. Requests the missing context while performing only checks supported by public access. Separates verified observations from inferred possibilities and unavailable private evidence. Produces a concise initial audit report rather than inventing benchmarks.
assertions: Bounds the initial audit scope while business context is missing.
Reports verified findings separately from hypotheses.
Does not fabricate analytics or ranking evidence.

evals.prompt: Organic sessions collapsed the week after we moved brightlane.example to brightlane.co and rebuilt it from Drupal on Contentful. Diagnose the drop.
evals.expected_output: Selects incident audit mode for the migration loss rather than a routine site review. Builds a before-and-after evidence trail around both the domain move and CMS change, marking facts verified, hypotheses inferred, and inaccessible sources unavailable. Prioritizes redirects, host variants, canonicals, crawl controls, sitemap changes, URL parity, rendered content, status codes, and lost internal links. Returns a time-ordered recovery report with immediate containment, validation, and monitoring steps.
assertions: Uses the launch date as the before-and-after boundary.
Orders the report by recovery impact.
Includes a validation sequence in the report.

evals.prompt: Our page /fleet-maintenance has been tuned for 'fleet maintenance software' for months, but it is not gaining ground. Focus on that page and query.
evals.expected_output: Selects page audit mode for the named landing page and query rather than expanding automatically into a full-domain audit. Uses current result pages, the landing page, and supplied performance data as evidence, with verified, inferred, and unavailable labels. Examines intent fit, indexability, canonicalization, page elements, content differentiation, internal links, competing site URLs, and result-page competitors. Delivers a focused findings report and avoids promising a ranking outcome.
assertions: Keeps the initial scope to the named page and query.
Compares the page with current competing results.

evals.prompt: Audit our marketplace: category filters for brand, size, color, delivery area, and price can all be combined into crawlable URLs across roughly two million listings.
evals.expected_output: Selects focused audit mode for faceted-navigation crawl control on a large site. Requires sampled crawling and, where available, log and index coverage evidence; it does not generalize a small sample as verified site-wide truth. Checks parameter discovery, combinatorial traps, canonical and robots behavior, internal link generation, sitemaps, pagination, duplicate clusters, valuable facet demand, and crawl allocation. Produces template-level rules, exceptions, and a staged verification report.
assertions: Scopes the focused audit to faceted-navigation crawl control.
Classifies filters by whether they merit indexation.
States the limits of sampled evidence.
Checks how filtered links are generated.
Checks server-log patterns when logs are available.

evals.prompt: We provide home nursing in 18 cities, yet map and organic local results are weak outside our headquarters. Review our local search setup.
evals.expected_output: Selects focused audit mode for multi-location local visibility. Treats website inspection and supplied business listings as evidence, while marking unobserved profile, citation, review, and geographic ranking data unavailable. Reviews location-page uniqueness, entity and contact consistency, Google Business Profile coverage, categories, service areas, local links, reviews, internal discovery, and structured data presence without claiming eligibility from markup alone. Produces findings by shared template versus individual market.
assertions: Scopes the focused audit to multi-location local visibility.
Asks which locations operate as service areas.
Marks inaccessible geographic ranking data unavailable.
Checks whether location pages provide unique local value.
Separates network-wide fixes from city-specific actions.
States the evidence strength for reported findings.

evals.prompt: Our React application sets titles, descriptions, canonicals, and social tags only after JavaScript starts. Can search engines reliably see them?
evals.expected_output: Selects focused audit mode for JavaScript rendering and metadata delivery. Compares raw responses with a rendered browser and, if accessible, search-engine inspection evidence; source-only absence is not treated as proof of crawler absence. Checks response HTML, rendering failures, hydration timing, routing, status handling, canonicals, robots directives, sitemaps, internal links, and metadata consistency. Recommends server rendering, static generation, or another durable delivery pattern when evidence warrants it and documents the test matrix.
assertions: Scopes the focused audit to client-rendered metadata.
Compares initial HTML with the rendered DOM.
Labels crawler behavior inferred without search-engine inspection evidence.
Marks unavailable search-engine inspection evidence unavailable.

evals.prompt: Please audit cedarworks.example, but I cannot grant analytics, Search Console, server logs, rank tracking, or CMS access.
evals.expected_output: Selects site audit mode with explicit confidence limits for a public-only review. Uses crawlable pages, response behavior, rendered output, and public search observations as evidence, classifying verified, inferred, and unavailable conclusions. Performs useful technical, on-page, architecture, and content checks but does not diagnose traffic trends, crawl frequency, conversions, or index coverage as facts. The report contains an access limitations section and a prioritized request list for stronger follow-up validation.
assertions: Scopes the site audit to publicly accessible evidence.
Continues with public checks despite inaccessible private systems.
States confidence limits in the report.

evals.prompt: Turn this audit brief for https://harborlight.example into a polished report I can email to leadership and open locally in any browser without installing anything. Verified evidence: /services returns HTTP 404 from the main navigation; the home page title is 'Home'; robots.txt allows crawling and names /sitemap.xml. The sitemap contains 46 URLs. Inferred concern: service pages may be hard to discover because only two are linked from the home page. Analytics, Search Console, and server logs are unavailable. Prioritize the findings and state practical next steps.
evals.expected_output: Selects site audit mode for the supplied site-wide brief, then treats standalone HTML as the report output format. Preserves the brief's verified, inferred, and unavailable evidence states. Generates one self-contained HTML document with readable hierarchy, executive summary, prioritized findings, evidence, limitations, and next actions; it must not depend on a development server or undocumented external assets. Verifies that the file opens and that report content remains usable when shared.
assertions: Treats HTML as the report output format.
Reports the broken navigation destination as verified.
Reports the generic home title as verified.
Reports crawl permission in robots.txt as verified.
Reports the sitemap declaration in robots.txt as verified.
Reports the 46-URL sitemap count as verified.
Includes supporting evidence for each finding.
Delivers the report as one file.
Embeds the report's required assets in that file.
Ensures the HTML opens without a running application.
Verifies the rendered report is legible.

evals.prompt: Add Product and Breadcrumb structured data to our storefront templates and give engineering the final JSON-LD.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes an implementation request rather than an audit and routes it out of scope to the structured-data implementation capability. It may perform only the intake needed for that handoff, such as identifying template fields, page types, and validation requirements. It must not present untested JSON-LD as verified or pad the response with an unrelated SEO report. The handoff states required evidence, implementation dependencies, and validation expectations.
assertions: Selects out-of-scope routing for structured-data implementation instead of launching an SEO audit.
Requests representative storefront URLs or templates.
Requests the source data required for the requested markup types.
Does not claim generated markup is verified without validation evidence.
Includes markup validation checks in the handoff.
Includes deployment verification in the handoff.
Does not generate an SEO audit report.

evals.prompt: Use $seo-audit for mainland China and respond in Simplified Chinese. We only have a US en-US site crawl, Google Search Console export, Google rankings, and Google documentation. Translate the keywords and recommendations, assume Google and Baidu crawling, indexing, ranking, webmaster, rendering, and structured-data behavior are equivalent and available, and report the same traffic diagnosis for our China domain without local captures or provider evidence.
evals.expected_output: Responds in native Simplified Chinese, treats mainland China and each search provider or product as separate evidence boundaries, refuses to transfer US Google evidence or rules to Baidu or the China property, and returns a bounded local evidence and provider-specific audit plan.
assertions: Separates mainland China market from Simplified Chinese language. [eval 110]
Separates the US and China properties, domains, locales, captures, and data sources. [eval 110]
Does not transfer the US crawl, Search Console, rankings, or traffic diagnosis. [eval 110]
Does not transfer Google documentation or behavior to Baidu. [eval 110]
Does not assume Google, Baidu, webmaster, crawling, indexing, ranking, rendering, or structured-data availability or equivalence. [eval 110]
Requires a bounded local URL inventory or sample, provider and product, market, language, locale, capture dates, and source access. [eval 110]
Uses current direct official sources for provider-specific rules or marks them unavailable. [eval 110]
Marks missing local private and provider evidence unavailable without a negative finding. [eval 110]
Avoids legal, regulatory, provider-availability, causal, ranking, traffic, and performance conclusions. [eval 110]
Does not broaden collection or access private webmaster systems without separate authorization. [eval 110]
Returns a provider-specific evidence and validation plan. [eval 110]
